title: Prepare the content model for additional certification tracks

acceptance_criteria.text: Remove vendor coupling that would misrepresent additional (non-CompTIA) tracks, so shared UI is driven by the manifest rather than hardcoded track names.
evidence: src/App.tsx: onboarding walkthrough copy derives the track list from the manifest (andList over available tracks); no longer hardcodes 'CompTIA' or A+/Network+/Security+
Verified live: step 2 reads 'move between A+ Track, Network+ Track, and Security+ Track' and updates as tracks change
Trademark disclaimer and track switcher already read vendor per-track (confirmed)

acceptance_criteria.text: Harden manifest validation for new-track safety: idPrefix must be unique across tracks (content ids are namespaced by prefix).
evidence: src/content/validate.ts + scripts/validate-content.mjs: reject a duplicate idPrefix with the owning track named
src/content/validate.test.ts: 'rejects two tracks sharing an idPrefix' (57 tests pass)
npm run validate:content still green for the three real tracks

acceptance_criteria.text: Document how to add a track for any vendor (not just CompTIA).
evidence: docs/certification-authoring.md: new 'Other Vendors (Non-CompTIA Tracks)' section (vendor flag, auto trademark/switcher, passThreshold guidance, single/multi exam, AWS example)
idPrefix-uniqueness rule documented; objective-mapping wording made vendor-neutral

acceptance_criteria.text: Authoring/advertising the first actual additional track is milestone 303's scope, not this preparation item.
evidence: 303-milestone-first-additional-certification covers choosing and authoring the first new track; 214 only makes the model/docs/validation ready.
